_comment: Copy this file to config/config.json and fill in YOUR numbers. config.json is git-ignored so your real data never gets committed. Every downstream script reads from here -- this is the single source of truth for identity, accounts, and assumptions. Canadian edition: RRSP/TFSA/RRIF/LIRA/FHSA/RESP accounts, CPP + OAS benefits, provincial tax. See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and docs/CANADA_RULES.md.
_language_note: Set the user-facing output language: 'en' (default) or 'fr'. Localizes the HTML dashboard and the spreadsheet labels (CLI output stays English). French is Canadian French (RPC=CPP, SV=OAS, REER=RRSP, CELI=TFSA, FERR=RRIF).

household.name: Your Household
household._province_note: Canada files individually (no joint return). Set your province/territory here (2-letter code: ON QC BC AB MB SK NS NB NL PE YT NT NU). The tax engine (engine/tax_ca.py) fully encodes all 10 provinces and 3 territories with 2025 brackets, BPA, and age/pension credits (Quebec also incl. its higher BPA, no surtax, the 16.5% federal abatement, and the HSF); an unrecognized code falls back to Ontario with a warning. Quebec residents: enter QPP in the cpp_monthly fields (taxed like CPP).

employer_stock._comment: Drives the company-health / RSU module. ticker can be any public company (use the NYSE listing for Canadian cross-listed names, e.g. RY, SHOP, ENB, for best yfinance coverage). sec_identity is only used for the US SEC EDGAR insider lookup -- Canadian-domiciled issuers do not file SEC Form 4, so that sub-signal returns empty; price/analyst signals still work. See docs/COMPANY_HEALTH.md.

government_benefits._comment: CPP and OAS are separate benefits with separate claim ages (set per member above). Enter the ESTIMATED monthly amount at the claim age you plan to use. CPP: claim 60-70 (standard 65). OAS: claim 65-70, and is subject to the OAS Recovery Tax (clawback) above the income threshold in assumptions. Quebec residents: enter QPP in the CPP field.

assumptions._comment: Returns/inflation default to FP Canada-style planning assumptions (lower than US defaults). oas_clawback_threshold is the Canadian analog of the US IRMAA ceiling -- the net-income line you manage RRSP-meltdown withdrawals to. Provincial figures are for your chosen province.
assumptions._bridge_note: Pre-CPP/OAS bridge: non-registered + TFSA drawn between retirement and the age CPP/OAS begin.
